title: Simira maxonii
taxonomy: Simira maxonii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Simira, which is a group of plants commonly known as the "Rubies of the forest". Within the Rubiaceae family, Simira maxonii is part of the large and diverse group of plants commonly referred to as "coffee and gardenias". This classification places Simira maxonii among plants that are known for their woody stems and small, often fragrant flowers. Further research is needed to determine the exact relationships between Simira maxonii and other plants within the Rubiaceae family.

intro: Simira maxonii, also known as the Simira tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Amazon region, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing pain and fever, as well as treating wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Simira maxonii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Simira maxonii include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, often used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, fever, and skin infections.
